title: Portal Frame vs Space Frame: 2026 Comparison
description: Compare portal frame and space frame steel structures: cost, span, construction time, applications. Make informed decision for your project.
content:
Choosing between portal frame and space frame is one of the first decisions in steel structure design. This comparison helps you make the right choice.

## Quick Comparison

| Factor | Portal Frame | Space Frame |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| **Span** | 15-60m | 20-120m+ |
| **Cost** | $180-350/m² | $350-600/m² |
| **Construction time** | 4-8 weeks | 8-12 weeks |
| **Column-free space** | Limited | Excellent |
| **Architectural flexibility** | Low | High |

## What is Portal Frame?

Portal frame is the most common steel structure for warehouses and factories.

### Key Features
- **Frame type**: Rigid frame with moment connections
- **Span**: 15-60m (optimal: 20-40m)
- **Eave height**: 6-12m typical
- **Roof slope**: 5-10%

### Pros
✅ Low cost  
✅ Fast construction  
✅ Simple design and erection  
✅ Easy to extend  

### Cons
❌ Limited span (needs columns for >60m)  
❌ Rectangular layout only  
❌ Less architectural flexibility  

### Best For
- Warehouses and logistics centers
- Factory buildings
- Agricultural buildings
- Small to medium commercial buildings

## What is Space Frame?

Space frame is a 3D truss system for large, column-free spaces.

### Key Features
- **Structure**: Interconnected struts in triangular pattern
- **Span**: 20-120m+ (no columns)
- **Shape**: Flat, curved, dome, pyramid
- **Load distribution**: Multi-directional

### Pros
✅ Enormous column-free spans  
✅ Architectural flexibility (curves, domes)  
✅ Excellent seismic performance  
✅ Lightweight relative to span  

### Cons
❌ Higher cost  
❌ Complex fabrication and erection  
❌ Longer design time  
❌ Requires specialized crew  

### Best For
- Airport terminals and hangars
- Sports arenas and stadiums
- Exhibition halls
- Shopping mall atriums
- Large religious buildings

## Head-to-Head Comparison

### 1. Cost

| Building Size | Portal Frame | Space Frame | Difference |
|--------------|-------------|-------------|-----------|
| 2,000m² | $360,000 | $700,000 | +94% |
| 5,000m² | $900,000 | $1,750,000 | +94% |
| 10,000m² | $1,800,000 | $3,500,000 | +94% |

**Winner**: Portal Frame (50% cheaper)

### 2. Span Capability

| Span | Portal Frame | Space Frame |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| 20m | ✅ Excellent | ✅ Good |
| 40m | ✅ Good | ✅ Excellent |
| 60m | ⚠️ Possible (heavy) | ✅ Excellent |
| 80m+ | ❌ Not recommended | ✅ Only option |

**Winner**: Space Frame (for >60m spans)

### 3. Construction Time

| Phase | Portal Frame | Space Frame |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| Design | 1-2 weeks | 3-4 weeks |
| Fabrication | 4-6 weeks | 6-8 weeks |
| Erection | 1-2 weeks | 2-4 weeks |
| **Total** | **6-10 weeks** | **11-16 weeks** |

**Winner**: Portal Frame (30% faster)

### 4. Maintenance

| Factor | Portal Frame | Space Frame |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| Access for painting | Easy | Difficult (many nodes) |
| Inspection | Simple | Complex |
| Coating life | 15-20 years | 10-15 years |

**Winner**: Portal Frame (easier maintenance)

### 5. Architectural Flexibility

| Feature | Portal Frame | Space Frame |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| Curves | ❌ Difficult | ✅ Easy |
| Domes | ❌ Impossible | ✅ Standard |
| Skylights | ✅ Possible | ✅ Excellent |
| Mixed use | ❌ Limited | ✅ Flexible |

**Winner**: Space Frame (architectural freedom)

## Decision Tree: Which to Choose?

### Choose Portal Frame if:
- ✅ Span < 60m
- ✅ Rectangular layout OK
- ✅ Budget is constraint
- ✅ Fast construction needed
- ✅ Standard appearance acceptable

### Choose Space Frame if:
- ✅ Span > 60m (or column-free needed)
- ✅ Architectural statement desired
- ✅ Mixed-use (exhibition + sports)
- ✅ Long-term investment (iconic building)
- ✅ Seismic zone (space frame performs better)

## Cost Optimization Tips

### Portal Frame
1. Optimize column spacing (6-7.5m optimal)
2. Use standard roof slope (5-7%)
3. Avoid heavy cranes (>10T increases cost significantly)
4. Consider pre-engineered options

### Space Frame
1. Standardize node types (reduce fabrication cost)
2. Use standard steel tubes (avoid custom sections)
3. Optimize for simple support conditions
4. Consider hybrid (space frame roof + portal frame walls)

## Case Study: 5,000m² Logistics Center

**Option A: Portal Frame**
- Span: 40m (3 bays of 13.3m)
- Cost: $180/m² × 5,000m² = $900,000
- Construction time: 10 weeks
- Columns: 2 rows (acceptable for logistics)

**Option B: Space Frame**
- Span: 50m (column-free)
- Cost: $350/m² × 5,000m² = $1,750,000
- Construction time: 14 weeks
- Columns: 0 (maximum space utilization)

**Decision**: Portal Frame (client chose cost savings over column-free space)

## FAQ: Portal Frame vs Space Frame

**Q: Can I convert portal frame to space frame later?**
A: No, they are fundamentally different systems. Choose correctly from start.

**Q: Is space frame stronger than portal frame?**
A: Not necessarily. Both can be designed for same loads. Space frame is more efficient for long spans.

**Q: Can space frame be dismantled and relocated?**
A: Possible but expensive. Bolted connections help, but node damage likely during dismantling.

**Q: Which has better resale value?**
A: Portal frame (more common, easier to repurpose). Space frame is often project-specific.

## Conclusion

**Choose Portal Frame** for cost-effective, fast construction of warehouses and factories.

**Choose Space Frame** for large, column-free spaces with architectural ambitions.
